triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Maximum Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application or request a significant reduction in the loan amount.
triageFlags.reasoning: The applicant's Debt-to-Income ratio is calculated as (Total Monthly Credit Commitments + Proposed Monthly Instalment) / Verified Net Monthly Income. With a net income of £500 and monthly commitments of £850, the DTI is 85%, which is significantly above the maximum allowable threshold of 45% for Tier A applicants as per policy section 3.1.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's Debt-to-Income ratio significantly exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application or request a significant increase in income or reduction in loan amount.
triageFlags.reasoning: The applicant's Debt-to-Income ratio is calculated as (Total Monthly Credit Commitments + Proposed Monthly Instalment) / Verified Net Monthly Income. With a net income of £500 and monthly commitments of £850, the DTI is 85%, which is well above the maximum allowable 45% for Tier A credit scores as per policy section 3.1.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Not Calculated
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio has not been calculated in the proposal.
triageFlags.recommendation: Calculate the Loan-to-Value ratio to ensure compliance with the policy limits.
triageFlags.reasoning: The Loan-to-Value ratio is a critical metric for assessing the financial risk of the loan. According to policy section 4.1, the LTV must not exceed 110% for Tier A applicants. The current proposal does not provide this value, which is necessary for compliance verification.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's Debt-to-Income ratio significantly exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Not Provided
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio is not calculated or provided in the application.
triageFlags.recommendation: Calculate the LTV ratio to ensure compliance with policy limits.
triageFlags.reasoning: Policy section 4.1 requires the LTV ratio to be calculated and within the specified limits for the applicant's credit tier. The absence of this calculation prevents proper assessment of the loan's compliance.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Policy Threshold

triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application or request a significant reduction in the loan amount.
triageFlags.reasoning: The applicant's DTI ratio is calculated as (Total Monthly Credit Commitments + Proposed Monthly Instalment) / Verified Net Monthly Income. With a net income of £500 and monthly commitments of £850, the DTI is 85%, which exceeds the maximum allowable threshold of 45% for Tier A applicants as per policy section 3.1.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Not Provided
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio is not calculated or provided in the application.
triageFlags.recommendation: Calculate and verify the LTV ratio to ensure compliance with policy limits.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy requires a maximum LTV of 110% for Tier A applicants. The LTV ratio is not provided in the application, which is a necessary metric for assessing the loan's compliance with policy.

content.reasoning: The analysis of the provided proposal revealed two significant issues. Firstly, the Debt-to-Income (DTI) ratio of the applicant is calculated to be 85%, which is significantly above the maximum allowable threshold of 45% for Tier A applicants as per the policy. This indicates a high risk of financial strain for the applicant. Secondly, the Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio, a critical metric for assessing the loan's compliance with policy, is not provided in the application. This omission prevents a complete evaluation of the loan's structure against policy requirements.
